name: Area Calculator
titleTag: Area Calculator - Square, Rectangle, Circle & More | Smart Calculator Pro
metaDescription: Calculate the area of squares, rectangles, circles, triangles, and more. Free area calculator for geometry and construction.
intro: Calculate the area of common shapes including squares, rectangles, circles, and triangles. Perfect for geometry homework, construction projects, and more.
shortDescription: Calculate area of squares, rectangles, circles, and triangles.
longDescription: This calculator computes the area of common geometric shapes using standard formulas. Simply select your shape and enter the required dimensions.
problemSolved: Helps users calculate area for various shapes and applications.

inputs.label: Shape
inputs.explanation: Select the shape you want to calculate the area for.

inputs.label: Width
inputs.explanation: The width of the rectangle or square.

inputs.label: Radius
inputs.explanation: The radius of the circle (distance from center to edge).

inputs.label: Base
inputs.explanation: The base length of the triangle.

outputs.label: Area
outputs.explanation: The calculated area in square feet.

formula.label: Area Formulas
formula.expression: Rectangle: A = l × w | Circle: A = πr² | Triangle: A = (b × h) / 2
formula.plainText: Each shape has its own formula: rectangle is length times width, circle is pi times radius squared, triangle is base times height divided by 2.

methodologyNotes: All measurements should be in the same unit for accurate results
Perimeter is calculated for rectangles and circles

example.scenario: Calculate the area of a rectangle with length 15 feet and width 10 feet.
example.resultSummary: The area is 150 square feet with a perimeter of 50 feet.

resultInterpretation.defaultText: Use the area result for purchasing materials, planning layouts, or solving geometry problems. Add 5-10% extra when ordering materials to account for waste and cutting.
warningMessages: Always measure carefully—small errors in measurement can lead to significant area calculation errors.
For construction projects, consider adding a waste factor of 5-10% to your calculated area.

faq.question: How do I calculate area?
faq.answer: Area is the size of a two-dimensional surface. The formula depends on the shape: rectangle = length × width, circle = π × radius², triangle = (base × height) ÷ 2.

faq.question: What is the difference between area and perimeter?
faq.answer: Area measures the space inside a shape (2D), while perimeter measures the distance around the outside edge (1D). Area is expressed in square units, perimeter in linear units.

faq.question: How many square feet are in an acre?
faq.answer: One acre equals 43,560 square feet. This is a useful conversion for land measurements.

disclaimer: This calculator provides mathematical results based on standard geometric formulas. For critical applications, verify your calculations.
